docs.title: DexComX
docs.text:
DexComX is a script-driven admin toolkit for BallsDex that lets owners create, update, filter, view, and remove model data quickly from Discord.

Credits

Originally created by Cayla (DexScript) — DexComX builds upon the original DexScript foundation with improved command parsing, speed aliases, and extension management.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Add to your config/extra.toml:

[[ballsdex.packages]]
location = "git+https://github.com/Haymooed/DexComX.git"
path = "dexscript_app"
enabled = true
editable = false

Then restart your BallsDex bot.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Speed Aliases: set, show, rm, ls, fields — faster commands
  • Scoped Commands: FILTER.UPDATE > BALL > REGIME > Democracy > Republic
  • Bulk Filter Operations: Mass update, delete, or view rows
  • Eval Presets: Save and run Python eval scripts
  • File System Tools: Read, write, clear, list, and delete files
  • Extension Manager: Install BallsDex packages directly from Discord
  • Batch Execution: Paste entire scripts with m.run prefix
  • Comment Support: Lines starting with -- are ignored

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
All commands are owner-only. Execute scripts with:

run <script>

or

m.run <script>

docs.title: Example: Create a Ball
docs.text:
CREATE > BALL > Germany
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > HEALTH > 100
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > ATTACK > 90
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > REGIME > Democracy

docs.title: Example: Bulk Operations
docs.text:
-- Change all Democracy regimes to Republic
FILTER.UPDATE > BALL > REGIME > Democracy > Republic

-- View all balls with health > 100
FILTER.VIEW > BALL > HEALTH > 100 > gt

docs.title: Example: Using Aliases
docs.text:
-- set is alias for UPDATE
set > BALL > France > HEALTH > 105

-- show is alias for VIEW
show > BALL > France

docs.title: Available Models
docs.text:
• BALL
  • REGIME
  • ECONOMY
  • SPECIAL

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Learn about Syntax
  • Explore Commands
  • Manage Extensions

docs.title: ATTRIBUTES
docs.text: Lists all editable attributes of a model.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: ATTRIBUTES > MODEL > FILTER(?)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description MODEL Required Model class FILTER Optional Filter type: null, valid

docs.title: List All Attributes
docs.text:
ATTRIBUTES > BALL

Output: 

BALL ATTRIBUTES:
- COUNTRY
- HEALTH
- ATTACK
- REGIME
- RARITY
- EMOJI_ID
...

docs.title: Filter Nullable Fields
docs.text:
ATTRIBUTES > BALL > null

Shows only fields that can be NULL.

docs.title: Filter Required Fields
docs.text:
ATTRIBUTES > BALL > valid

Shows only required (non-nullable) fields.

docs.title: Using Alias
docs.text:
-- fields is alias for ATTRIBUTES
fields > BALL

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• UPDATE - Update attributes
  • VIEW - View attribute values
  • CREATE - Create instances

docs.title: CREATE
docs.text: Creates a new model instance with default values for required fields.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: CREATE > MODEL > IDENTIFIER

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description MODEL Required Model class: BALL, REGIME, ECONOMY, SPECIAL IDENTIFIER Required Name/identifier for the new instance

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Creates a new instance of the specified model
  • Auto-populates required fields with default values
  • Returns confirmation message

docs.title: Create a Ball
docs.text:
CREATE > BALL > Germany

Output: 

Created `Germany` ball

docs.title: Create a Regime
docs.text: CREATE > REGIME > Republic

docs.title: Create Multiple Instances
docs.text:
CREATE > BALL > Spain
CREATE > BALL > Italy
CREATE > BALL > Portugal

docs.title: After Creation
docs.text:
After creating an instance, you'll typically want to update its attributes:

CREATE > BALL > NewCountry
UPDATE > BALL > NewCountry > HEALTH > 100
UPDATE > BALL > NewCountry > ATTACK > 90
UPDATE > BALL > NewCountry > REGIME > Democracy
UPDATE > BALL > NewCountry > RARITY > 1.0

docs.title: Default Values
docs.text:
DexComX automatically sets safe defaults for required fields:

  • Numeric fields: 1 (integers), 1.0 (floats)
  • Text fields: "placeholder"
  • Boolean fields: False
  • Foreign keys: First available instance from related model
  • JSON fields: {}

docs.title: Template Helper
docs.text:
For complex models like BALL, use the template helper:

TEMPLATE > CREATE > BALL > Germany

This generates a full setup template with all common fields.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• UPDATE - Modify instance attributes
  • DELETE - Remove instances
  • VIEW - Inspect instance data
  • TEMPLATE.CREATE - Generate setup templates

docs.title: DELETE
docs.text: Deletes a model instance.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: DELETE > MODEL > IDENTIFIER

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description MODEL Required Model class: BALL, REGIME, ECONOMY, SPECIAL IDENTIFIER Required Name/identifier of the instance to delete

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Permanently removes the specified instance
  • Cannot be undone
  • Returns confirmation message

docs.title: Delete a Ball
docs.text:
DELETE > BALL > Germany

Output: 

Deleted `Germany` ball

docs.title: Delete Multiple Instances
docs.text:
DELETE > BALL > OldBall1
DELETE > BALL > OldBall2
DELETE > BALL > OldBall3

docs.title: Using Aliases
docs.text:
-- rm is alias for DELETE
rm > BALL > OldBall

-- del is also an alias
del > BALL > TestBall

docs.title: Safety Tips
docs.text:
Permanent Deletion

Deleted instances cannot be recovered. Always verify the identifier before deleting.

docs.title: Verify Before Deleting
docs.text:
-- Check the ball exists first
VIEW > BALL > OldBall

-- Then delete
DELETE > BALL > OldBall

docs.title: Bulk Deletion
docs.text:
For deleting multiple instances matching a condition, use FILTER.DELETE:

-- Delete all balls with Communist regime
FILTER.DELETE > BALL > REGIME > Communist

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
If the instance doesn't exist:

ERROR: 'NonExistentBall' does not exist.

DexComX provides autocorrect suggestions if you mistype.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• CREATE - Create new instances
  • FILTER.DELETE - Bulk deletion
  • VIEW - Verify instance before deleting

docs.title: EVAL.LIST
docs.text: Lists all saved eval presets.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: EVAL.LIST

docs.title: List Presets
docs.text:
EVAL.LIST

Output: 

calculate_stats.py
export_data.py
update_regimes.py

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Shows all preset names
  • Presets stored in eval_presets/ directory
  • Returns "no presets" if folder is empty

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• EVAL.SAVE - Create presets
  • EVAL.RUN - Execute presets
  • EVAL.REMOVE - Delete presets

docs.title: EVAL.REMOVE
docs.text: Deletes a saved eval preset.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: EVAL.REMOVE > NAME

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description NAME Required Preset name to remove

docs.title: Remove a Preset
docs.text: EVAL.REMOVE > old_script

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Permanently deletes the preset file
  • Cannot be undone
  • Preset must exist

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• EVAL.SAVE - Create presets
  • EVAL.LIST - View all presets
  • EVAL.RUN - Execute presets

docs.title: EVAL.RUN
docs.text: Executes a saved eval preset.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: EVAL.RUN > NAME

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description NAME Required Preset name to execute

docs.title: Run a Preset
docs.text: EVAL.RUN > calculate_stats

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Executes Python code from the preset
  • Uses bot's eval command internally
  • Shows ✅ on success, error message on failure

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• EVAL.SAVE - Create presets
  • EVAL.LIST - View all presets
  • EVAL.REMOVE - Delete presets

docs.title: EVAL.SAVE
docs.text: Saves a Python eval preset for later execution.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: EVAL.SAVE > NAME

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description NAME Required Preset name (max 100 chars)

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Run EVAL.SAVE > my_preset
  2. Bot asks: "Please send the eval command below..."
  3. Send your Python eval code
  4. Preset is saved

docs.title: Save a Preset
docs.text:
run EVAL.SAVE > calculate_stats

Then send: 

total_balls = await Ball.objects.count()
print(f"Total balls: {total_balls}")

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Preset saved to eval_presets/NAME.py
  • 100-character name limit
  • Overwrites not allowed (prevents accidents)

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• EVAL.RUN - Execute saved presets
  • EVAL.LIST - View all presets
  • EVAL.REMOVE - Delete presets

docs.title: FILE.CLEAR
docs.text: Clears all content from a file (makes it empty).

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: FILE.CLEAR > PATH

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description PATH Required File path to clear

docs.title: Clear Log File
docs.text: FILE.CLEAR > ./logs/debug.log

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Empties the file (0 bytes)
  • File must already exist
  • Does not delete the file

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• FILE.DELETE - Delete files completely
  • FILE.WRITE - Write new content

docs.title: FILE.DELETE
docs.text: Deletes a file or directory permanently.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: FILE.DELETE > PATH

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description PATH Required File or directory path

docs.title: Delete File
docs.text: FILE.DELETE > ./temp/old_data.json

docs.title: Delete Directory
docs.text: FILE.DELETE > ./temp

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Permanently deletes files or directories
  • Directories are deleted recursively
  • Cannot be undone

Warning

This action is permanent. Always verify the path before deleting.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• FILE.CLEAR - Clear file without deleting
  • FILE.LISTDIR - List files

docs.title: FILE.LISTDIR
docs.text: Lists all files and directories in a path.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: FILE.LISTDIR > PATH(?)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description PATH Optional Directory path (defaults to current directory)

docs.title: List Current Directory
docs.text: FILE.LISTDIR

docs.title: List Specific Directory
docs.text: FILE.LISTDIR > ./data

docs.title: Using Alias
docs.text:
-- ls is alias for LISTDIR
FILE.ls > ./config

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Shows files and subdirectories
  • Returns as paginated list
  • Respects Discord message limits

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• FILE.READ - Read file contents
  • FILE.DELETE - Delete files

docs.title: FILE.READ
docs.text: Sends a file as a Discord attachment.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: FILE.READ > PATH

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description PATH Required File path relative to bot directory

docs.title: Read Config File
docs.text: FILE.READ > ./config.json

docs.title: Read From Subdirectory
docs.text: FILE.READ > ./data/export.csv

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Sends file as Discord attachment
  • File must exist and be readable
  • Respects Discord file size limits (25MB)

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• FILE.WRITE - Write to files
  • FILE.LISTDIR - List directory contents

docs.title: FILE.WRITE
docs.text: Writes Discord attachment content to a file.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
FILE.WRITE > PATH

Requires: Attach a file to the message

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description PATH Required Destination file path

docs.title: Write to Config
docs.text:
FILE.WRITE > ./config.json

(Attach config.json file to message)

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Overwrites existing file
  • Creates file if it doesn't exist
  • File content comes from Discord attachment

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• FILE.READ - Read files
  • FILE.CLEAR - Clear file contents

docs.title: FILTER.DELETE
docs.text: Mass delete all instances matching a condition.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: FILTER.DELETE > MODEL > ATTRIBUTE > VALUE > OPERATOR(?)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description MODEL Required Model class ATTRIBUTE Required Field to match on VALUE Required Value to match OPERATOR Optional Comparison: gt, lt, gte, lte

docs.title: Simple Delete
docs.text:
-- Delete all balls with Communist regime
FILTER.DELETE > BALL > REGIME > Communist

docs.title: With Operator
docs.text:
-- Delete all balls with health < 50
FILTER.DELETE > BALL > HEALTH > 50 > lt

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• FILTER.UPDATE - Bulk updates
  • FILTER.VIEW - View before deleting
  • DELETE - Delete single instance

docs.title: FILTER.UPDATE
docs.text: Mass update all instances matching a condition.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: FILTER.UPDATE > MODEL > ATTRIBUTE > OLD_VALUE > NEW_VALUE > OPERATOR(?)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description MODEL Required Model class ATTRIBUTE Required Field to match on OLD_VALUE Required Value to match NEW_VALUE Required New value to set OPERATOR Optional Comparison: gt, lt, gte, lte

docs.title: Simple Update
docs.text:
-- Change all Democracy regimes to Republic
FILTER.UPDATE > BALL > REGIME > Democracy > Republic

docs.title: With Operator
docs.text:
-- Set all balls with health > 100 to 100
FILTER.UPDATE > BALL > HEALTH > 100 > 100 > gt

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• FILTER.DELETE - Bulk deletion
  • FILTER.VIEW - View matching instances
  • UPDATE - Update single instance

docs.title: FILTER.VIEW
docs.text: Display all instances matching a condition.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: FILTER.VIEW > MODEL > ATTRIBUTE > VALUE > OPERATOR(?)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description MODEL Required Model class ATTRIBUTE Required Field to match on VALUE Required Value to match OPERATOR Optional Comparison: gt, lt, gte, lte

docs.title: Simple View
docs.text:
-- View all balls with Democracy regime
FILTER.VIEW > BALL > REGIME > Democracy

docs.title: With Operator
docs.text:
-- View all balls with health > 100
FILTER.VIEW > BALL > HEALTH > 100 > gt

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• FILTER.UPDATE - Bulk updates
  • FILTER.DELETE - Bulk deletion
  • VIEW - View single instance

docs.title: TEMPLATE.CREATE
docs.text: Generates a pre-filled template for creating model instances.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: TEMPLATE.CREATE > MODEL > IDENTIFIER(?)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description MODEL Required Model class (currently only BALL supported) IDENTIFIER Optional Name for the instance (defaults to ...)

docs.title: Generate Ball Template
docs.text:
TEMPLATE.CREATE > BALL > Germany

Output: 

CREATE > BALL > Germany
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > REGIME > ...
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > HEALTH > ...
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > ATTACK > ...
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > RARITY > ...
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > EMOJI_ID > ...
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > CREDITS > ...
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > CAPACITY_NAME > ...
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > CAPACITY_DESCRIPTION > ...

docs.title: Generic Template
docs.text:
TEMPLATE.CREATE > BALL

Uses ... as placeholder for all values.

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Generates ready-to-paste script
  • Includes all common attributes
  • Replace ... with actual values

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Generate template
  2. Copy output
  3. Replace ... placeholders
  4. Paste into m.run batch execution

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• CREATE - Create instances
  • UPDATE - Update attributes
  • Batch Execution - Run templates

docs.title: UPDATE
docs.text: Updates a model instance's attribute value.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: UPDATE > MODEL > IDENTIFIER > ATTRIBUTE > VALUE

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description MODEL Required Model class: BALL, REGIME, ECONOMY, SPECIAL IDENTIFIER Required Name/identifier of the instance ATTRIBUTE Required Field name to update VALUE Required New value for the attribute

docs.title: Update Ball Health
docs.text: UPDATE > BALL > France > HEALTH > 100

docs.title: Update Multiple Attributes
docs.text:
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > HEALTH > 105
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > ATTACK > 90
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > REGIME > Democracy
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > RARITY > 1.5

docs.title: Using Alias
docs.text:
-- set is alias for UPDATE
set > BALL > France > HEALTH > 105

docs.title: Update Foreign Keys
docs.text:
-- Link a ball to a regime
UPDATE > BALL > France > REGIME > Democracy

docs.title: Bulk Updates
docs.text:
For updating multiple instances at once, use FILTER.UPDATE:

FILTER.UPDATE > BALL > REGIME > Democracy > Republic

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• CREATE - Create instances
  • VIEW - View current values
  • FILTER.UPDATE - Bulk updates

docs.title: VIEW
docs.text: Displays an attribute or all attributes of a model instance.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: VIEW > MODEL > IDENTIFIER > ATTRIBUTE(?)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description MODEL Required Model class IDENTIFIER Required Instance name ATTRIBUTE Optional Specific field to view (omit to see all)

docs.title: View All Attributes
docs.text:
VIEW > BALL > France

Output: 

country: France
health: 100
attack: 90
regime_id: 1
rarity: 1.0
...

docs.title: View Specific Attribute
docs.text:
VIEW > BALL > France > HEALTH

Output: 

100

docs.title: Using Alias
docs.text:
-- show is alias for VIEW
show > BALL > France
show > BALL > France > HEALTH

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• UPDATE - Modify values
  • ATTRIBUTES - List available fields
  • FILTER.VIEW - View multiple instances

docs.title: Add Extension
docs.text: Install a new BallsDex package from a git repository.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: m.dexadmin extension add <url>

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description url Required Git repository URL (HTTPS or SSH)

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
1. Validates the git URL
  2. Extracts package name from URL
  3. Downloads package via pip
  4. Adds entry to config/extra.toml
  5. Attempts to load the extension
  6. Shows installation status

docs.title: Install from GitHub (HTTPS)
docs.text: m.dexadmin extension add https://github.com/Haymooed/BallsDex-Merchant-Package.git

docs.title: Install from GitHub (SSH)
docs.text: m.dexadmin extension add git@github.com:User/Package.git

docs.title: Install from GitLab
docs.text: m.dexadmin extension add https://gitlab.com/user/package.git

docs.title: Successful Installation
docs.text:
📦 Installing Extension
Installing `ballsdex_merchant_package` from:
https://github.com/Haymooed/BallsDex-Merchant-Package.git

✅ Extension Installed
Successfully installed and loaded `ballsdex_merchant_package`

URL: https://github.com/Haymooed/BallsDex-Merchant-Package.git
Path: ballsdex_merchant_package

Extension is now active. Restart may be required for full functionality.

docs.title: Already Installed
docs.text: ⚠️ Package `ballsdex_merchant_package` is already installed from this URL.

docs.title: Installation Failed
docs.text:
❌ Installation Failed
[error details]

docs.title: What Gets Added to Config
docs.text:
The command automatically adds to config/extra.toml:

[[ballsdex.packages]]
location = "git+https://github.com/Haymooed/BallsDex-Merchant-Package.git"
path = "ballsdex_merchant_package"
enabled = true
editable = false

docs.title: Package Naming
docs.text:
Package names are auto-generated from the URL:

  • https://github.com/User/My-Package.git → my_package
  • Hyphens and spaces become underscores
  • Converted to lowercase

docs.title: Check if Loaded
docs.text:
m.dexadmin extension list

Should show your new package with status ✅ Enabled.

docs.title: If Not Working
docs.text:
Some packages require a full bot restart:

  1. Stop the bot
  2. Restart the bot
  3. Extension should now be active

docs.title: Invalid URL Format
docs.text:
❌ Invalid URL format. Use HTTPS or SSH git URLs.
Example: `https://github.com/User/Package.git`

Solution: Ensure you're using a valid git URL starting with http or git@.

docs.title: Installation Timeout
docs.text:
⏱️ Installation Timeout
Installation took too long. The package may still be installing in the background.

Solution: Wait a few minutes and check m.dexadmin extension list.

docs.title: Permission Errors
docs.text:
❌ Installation Error
[Permission denied]

Solution: Ensure the bot process has write access to the config directory.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Check before installing - Run m.dexadmin extension list first
  2. Test on development bot - Try new packages in a test environment
  3. Read package docs - Check the package's README for special requirements
  4. Monitor logs - Watch bot logs during installation for errors

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Remove Extension - Uninstall packages
  • List Extensions - View installed packages
  • Update Extension - Update to latest version

docs.title: List Extensions
docs.text: View all installed BallsDex extensions.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: m.dexadmin extension list

docs.title: List Installed Extensions
docs.text: m.dexadmin extension list

docs.title: With Packages Installed
docs.text:
📦 Installed Extensions
Found 2 package(s)

`ballsdex_merchant_package`
Status: ✅ Enabled
URL: https://github.com/Haymooed/BallsDex-Merchant-Package.git

`ballsdex_art_package`
Status: ✅ Enabled
URL: https://github.com/Caylies/Art-BD-Package.git

docs.title: No Packages
docs.text:
📦 No extensions installed.
Use `m.dexadmin extension add <url>` to install one.

docs.title: Information Shown
docs.text:
• Package name (path)
  • Status (enabled/disabled)
  • Git repository URL

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Add Extension - Install new packages
  • Remove Extension - Uninstall packages
  • Update Extension - Update packages

docs.title: Extension Manager
docs.text: DexComX includes a built-in extension manager that lets you install, update, and remove BallsDex packages directly from Discord.

docs.title: What is the Extension Manager?
docs.text:
The extension manager allows you to:

  • Install packages from GitHub repositories
  • Manage installed packages without editing config files
  • Update packages to their latest versions
  • Remove packages cleanly

docs.title: Why Use It?
docs.text:
Before: 

# Manually edit config/extra.toml
[[ballsdex.packages]]
location = "git+https://github.com/User/Package.git"
path = "package_name"
enabled = true
editable = false

Now: 

m.dexadmin extension add https://github.com/User/Package.git

docs.title: Available Commands
docs.text:
All extension manager commands are under the m.dexadmin extension group:

  • add - Install a new extension
  • remove - Remove an installed extension
  • list - Show all installed extensions
  • update - Update an extension to latest version

docs.title: Install a Package
docs.text:
m.dexadmin extension add https://github.com/Haymooed/BallsDex-Merchant-Package.git

DexComX will:

  1. Download the package via pip
  2. Add it to config/extra.toml
  3. Attempt to load the extension
  4. Show success or error message

docs.title: List Installed Packages
docs.text:
m.dexadmin extension list

Shows all packages with their URLs and status.

docs.title: Update a Package
docs.text:
m.dexadmin extension update ballsdex_merchant_package

Pulls the latest version and reinstalls.

docs.title: Remove a Package
docs.text:
m.dexadmin extension remove ballsdex_merchant_package

Removes from config and unloads the extension.

docs.title: Installation Flow
docs.text:
1. User runs m.dexadmin extension add <url>
  2. DexComX validates the git URL
  3. Package is downloaded via pip install git+<url>
  4. Entry is added to config/extra.toml
  5. Extension is loaded (if possible)
  6. Success/failure message is shown

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
All changes are saved to config/extra.toml in the standard BallsDex format:

[[ballsdex.packages]]
location = "git+https://github.com/User/Package.git"
path = "package_name"
enabled = true
editable = false

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Bot owner permissions (all m.dexadmin commands are owner-only)
  • Git-based package URLs (GitHub, GitLab, etc.)
  • Package must follow BallsDex package structure

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
• Some packages may require a bot restart to fully activate
  • Only works with git URLs (not local paths)
  • Package names are auto-generated from URLs

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Always list first - Run m.dexadmin extension list before adding
  2. Test in development - Try new packages on a test bot first
  3. Keep updated - Regularly update packages with m.dexadmin extension update
  4. Remove unused - Clean up packages you're not using

docs.title: Package won't load
docs.text: Try restarting the bot. Some packages need a full restart to activate.

docs.title: "Package already installed"
docs.text: Use m.dexadmin extension list to see what's installed, or use update instead of add.

docs.title: Permission errors
docs.text: Ensure you're the bot owner. All m.dexadmin commands are owner-only.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Add Extension - Install packages
  • Remove Extension - Uninstall packages
  • List Extensions - View installed packages
  • Update Extension - Update to latest versions

docs.title: Remove Extension
docs.text: Remove an installed BallsDex package.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: m.dexadmin extension remove <package_name>

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description package_name Required Name of the installed package

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
1. Removes package from config/extra.toml
  2. Attempts to unload the extension
  3. Shows confirmation message

docs.title: Remove a Package
docs.text: m.dexadmin extension remove ballsdex_merchant_package

docs.title: Successful Removal
docs.text:
✅ Extension Removed
Removed `ballsdex_merchant_package` from configuration.

Note:
The package files remain installed. Restart the bot to fully unload.

docs.title: Package Not Found
docs.text:
❌ Package `unknown_package` not found in configuration.
Use `m.dexadmin extension list` to see installed packages.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Package files remain in Python environment
  • Restart bot to fully unload extension
  • Use m.dexadmin extension list to find exact package names

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Add Extension - Install packages
  • List Extensions - View installed packages
  • Update Extension - Update packages

docs.title: Update Extension
docs.text: Update an installed extension to the latest version.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: m.dexadmin extension update <package_name>

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description package_name Required Name of the package to update

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
1. Pulls latest version from git repository
  2. Reinstalls using pip with --force-reinstall
  3. Shows update status

docs.title: Update a Package
docs.text: m.dexadmin extension update ballsdex_merchant_package

docs.title: Successful Update
docs.text:
✅ Extension Updated
Successfully updated `ballsdex_merchant_package`

Action Required:
Restart the bot to apply updates.

docs.title: Package Not Found
docs.text:
❌ Package `unknown_package` not found.
Use `m.dexadmin extension list` to see installed packages.

docs.title: Update Failed
docs.text:
❌ Update Failed
[error details]

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Always restart bot after updating
  • Updates pull from the same URL used during installation
  • Use m.dexadmin extension list to find package names

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Check for updates regularly - Keep packages up to date
  2. Test in development first - Try updates on test bot
  3. Restart after updating - Required for changes to take effect

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Add Extension - Install packages
  • Remove Extension - Uninstall packages
  • List Extensions - View installed packages

docs.title: Aliases
docs.text: DexComX supports built-in aliases for faster scripting.

docs.title: Available Aliases
docs.text: Alias Full Command set UPDATE show VIEW fields ATTRIBUTES rm DELETE del DELETE ls LISTDIR

docs.title: set → UPDATE
docs.text:
-- Instead of:
UPDATE > BALL > France > HEALTH > 105

-- You can use:
set > BALL > France > HEALTH > 105

docs.title: show → VIEW
docs.text:
-- Instead of:
VIEW > BALL > France

-- You can use:
show > BALL > France

docs.title: rm / del → DELETE
docs.text:
-- Instead of:
DELETE > BALL > OldBall

-- You can use:
rm > BALL > OldBall
-- or:
del > BALL > OldBall

docs.title: ls → LISTDIR
docs.text:
-- Instead of:
FILE > LISTDIR > ./

-- You can use:
FILE.ls > ./

docs.title: fields → ATTRIBUTES
docs.text:
-- Instead of:
ATTRIBUTES > BALL

-- You can use:
fields > BALL

docs.title: Combining with Scoped Mode
docs.text:
Aliases work with scoped commands too:

-- FILE.ls instead of FILE.LISTDIR
FILE.ls > ./config

-- FILTER.rm instead of FILTER.DELETE
FILTER.rm > BALL > REGIME > Communist

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Faster typing - Less characters to type
  • More readable - Familiar Unix-like commands (ls, rm)
  • Backward compatible - Full commands still work

Tip

Use aliases in interactive sessions for speed, and full commands in scripts for clarity.

docs.title: Batch Execution
docs.text: Execute multiple DexComX commands in a single message using batch mode.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
m.run
COMMAND1 > ARG1 > ARG2
COMMAND2 > ARG1 > ARG2
COMMAND3 > ARG1 > ARG2

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text: Prefix your multi-line script with m.run (or m.run) and DexComX will execute each line sequentially.

docs.title: Basic Batch Script
docs.text:
m.run
CREATE > BALL > Germany
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > HEALTH > 100
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > ATTACK > 90
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > REGIME > Democracy

docs.title: With Comments
docs.text:
m.run
-- Create new balls
CREATE > BALL > France
CREATE > BALL > Germany

-- Configure France
UPDATE > BALL > France > HEALTH > 100
UPDATE > BALL > France > ATTACK > 85

-- Configure Germany
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > HEALTH > 105
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > ATTACK > 90

docs.title: Mixed Commands
docs.text:
m.run
-- Filter operations
FILTER.UPDATE > BALL > REGIME > Democracy > Republic
FILTER.DELETE > BALL > REGIME > Communist

-- Create new ball
CREATE > BALL > NewCountry

-- File operations
FILE.READ > ./config.json

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: If any command in the batch fails: - DexComX continues executing remaining commands - All errors are collected and shown at the end - Successful commands show ✅ reaction

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Paste entire scripts - Copy/paste from text files
  • Atomic operations - Run related commands together
  • Script sharing - Share full workflows with others
  • Version control - Save scripts in git

docs.title: Alternative: Single Command Mode
docs.text:
Without m.run, send one command per message:

run CREATE > BALL > Germany

run UPDATE > BALL > Germany > HEALTH > 100

Batch mode is faster for multiple commands.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
1. Test individual commands first - Verify syntax before batching
  2. Use comments liberally - Document what each section does
  3. Save scripts as files - Keep reusable scripts in your project
  4. Check for errors - Review all error messages at the end

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Comments - Document your batch scripts
  • Syntax Overview - Command structure
  • Aliases - Speed up batch scripts

docs.title: Comments
docs.text: DexComX supports single-line comments using -- syntax.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: -- This is a comment

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: Comments are lines that start with -- and are completely ignored during script execution.

docs.title: Inline Documentation
docs.text:
-- Create a new ball for Germany
CREATE > BALL > Germany

-- Set health to 100
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > HEALTH > 100

docs.title: Section Headers
docs.text:
-- ==========================================
-- BALL SETUP SCRIPT
-- ==========================================

-- Create balls
CREATE > BALL > France
CREATE > BALL > Germany
CREATE > BALL > Spain

-- Configure health values
UPDATE > BALL > France > HEALTH > 100
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > HEALTH > 105
UPDATE > BALL > Spain > HEALTH > 95

docs.title: Disable Commands Temporarily
docs.text:
CREATE > BALL > TestBall
UPDATE > BALL > TestBall > HEALTH > 100
-- UPDATE > BALL > TestBall > ATTACK > 50
UPDATE > BALL > TestBall > REGIME > Democracy

The commented line will be skipped.

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Document your scripts - Explain what each section does
  • Disable commands - Comment out lines instead of deleting them
  • Add headers - Organize long scripts into sections
  • Leave notes - Remind yourself why you made certain changes

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Comments must start with -- at the beginning of the line
  • Leading whitespace before -- is allowed
  • Everything after -- on that line is ignored
  • Multi-line comments are not supported (use multiple -- lines)

docs.title: Combined with Batch Execution
docs.text:
m.run
-- Batch script with comments
CREATE > BALL > Germany
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > HEALTH > 100
-- This next line is disabled
-- DELETE > BALL > OldBall
UPDATE > BALL > Germany > ATTACK > 90

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Batch Execution - Run multi-line scripts
  • Syntax Overview - Command structure

docs.title: Syntax Overview
docs.text: DexComX uses a simple, command-based syntax with the > separator.

docs.title: Basic Format
docs.text: COMMAND > ARGUMENT1 > ARGUMENT2 > ARGUMENT3

docs.title: Scoped Mode
docs.text:
Use dot-notation to scope to a command class:

FILTER.UPDATE > BALL > REGIME > Democracy > Republic
FILE.READ > ./config.json

docs.title: Slash-Style
docs.text:
You can prefix commands with / for readability:

/show > BALL > France > HEALTH
/rm > BALL > OldBall

docs.title: Global Commands
docs.text:
UPDATE > BALL > France > HEALTH > 100
VIEW > BALL > France
DELETE > BALL > OldBall

docs.title: Scoped Commands
docs.text:
FILTER.UPDATE > BALL > REGIME > Democracy > Republic
FILE.READ > ./settings.json
EVAL.RUN > my_preset

docs.title: Models
docs.text:
DexComX works with these BallsDex models:

  • BALL - Country balls/collectibles
  • REGIME - Political regimes
  • ECONOMY - Economic systems
  • SPECIAL - Special items

docs.title: Optional Arguments
docs.text:
Some commands have optional arguments marked with (?):

VIEW > BALL > France > HEALTH(?)
ATTRIBUTES > BALL > FILTER(?)

If omitted, the command uses default behavior.

docs.title: Simple Update
docs.text: UPDATE > BALL > France > HEALTH > 100

docs.title: Scoped Filter
docs.text: FILTER.VIEW > BALL > REGIME > Democracy

docs.title: File Operation
docs.text: FILE.READ > ./config.json

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Aliases - Speed up commands with shortcuts
  • Comments - Document your scripts
  • Batch Execution - Run multiple commands at once
